mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 315.0 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 315.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a resilient labor market, supporting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in >55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend pace signals expanding economic output and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% indicates immediate supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods.
